It also appears to stop at exactly eight queries despite additional strong Chinese-language variants being relevant, which violates the explicit recall requirement. It does not include the recommended expanded exam name "Hanyu Shuiping Kaoshi"; "hanyu" alone is not an adequate substitute. It also omits useful variants like the exact title "Mandarin Chinese" and Simplified "普通话". The lowercase "hsk" is probably usable for search, but it does not preserve the standard acronym styling.

Add more high-recall, concise queries such as "Mandarin Chinese", "普通话", "普通話", "漢語", "Hanyu Shuiping Kaoshi", and possibly "Chinese Mandarin" while keeping out unrelated languages and generic filler terms.
